data.title: ECHO CHAMBER
data.description:
An interactive digital experience that visualises the architecture of online discourse. Users speak into their device and watch as their words fragment, multiply, and distort across a three-dimensional space — a visceral metaphor for how information mutates in digital echo chambers.

Built with WebGL and the Web Audio API, the piece renders real-time audio analysis as particle systems that collide, merge, and fracture. Each word spoken generates a unique visual signature that interacts with the traces left by previous visitors, creating a collective tapestry of accumulated voice.

The project was awarded a Webby Award for Best Net Art and featured at Ars Electronica 2024.

images.alt: Exhibition view of the interactive display
